calls.quote: Him stock is up 40% aftermarket today.

calls.quote: Hims and MD are tied for my largest positions
calls.summary: The creator mentions MD as one of his largest positions, indicating a bullish stance.

calls.quote: HIMS is down 23%! OMG! Kevans, what do I do? Do I sell? Relax! ... The only time I will sell my position is if the fundamentals in the company have changed and I have a more bearish outlook on the vision for the future.
calls.summary: The creator advises holding HIMS despite a 23% drop, citing long-term investing philosophy and personal risk tolerance.

calls.quote: I personally plan on holding for the long term because Nebius is a winner and I don't want to sell my winners.
calls.summary: The creator is bullish on Nebius, citing its AI cloud infrastructure, strong financials, and scaling with NVIDIA GPUs, and plans to hold long-term.

calls.quote: I usually never FOMO into stocks like this and my investing strategy is to buy the dips and accumulate when the prices are lower.
calls.summary: The creator is buying ServiceNow (NOW) at current price, citing it is down 43% from all-time highs and he is accumulating despite FOMO.

calls.quote: that stock is Reddit, ticker RDDT
calls.summary: Creator bought Reddit stock, citing strong revenue growth, high gross margins, low capex, and AI data moat, while acknowledging Google algorithm risk.

calls.quote: Iron is my favorite AI stock right now and it's carrying my portfolio. I am personally not taking profits yet because I see a bigger growth story playing out and I'm investing in Iren for the long term.

calls.quote: I think it's about damn time to buy this dip.

calls.quote: I anticipate there to be about a 10 to 20% dip before we skyrocket and really finish off the bull market.

calls.quote: I personally think there's more upside in buying altcoins like Ethereum... those things are going to be the ones 10xing your money.

calls.quote: I'm double downing and buying even more because I have such high conviction.

calls.quote: I'm backing that vision and willing to take the risk because I believe in the potential upside more than the downside right now at these current price levels.

calls.quote: HIMS is my second largest stock holding after AMD. I entered this stock at $15 and I rode it all the way up to $70 before it came crashing back down towards $15, and today I'm holding at $29.

calls.quote: I just spent $2,000 on a brand new stock that I think has the potential to 3x in the near future. That stock is Grab and they're basically the Uber of Southeast Asia.
calls.summary: Creator bought Grab, citing its dominance in Southeast Asia as the 'Uber of Southeast Asia', strong financials with 20% revenue growth, institutional buying, and founder-led CEO, expecting a potential 3x return.

calls.quote: First up is Hims and Hers, which makes up 40% of my portfolio. They're a telehealth company that sells medicine and they solve a huge insecurity, how you feel and how you look. And they're also led by a visionary founder.

calls.quote: Next up, I got SoFi, who's modernizing the banking experience. The younger generation is flocking away from brick and mortar established banks and it's for a good reason.
calls.summary: The creator is bullish on SoFi for modernizing banking and attracting younger customers, and plans to buy during the dip.

calls.quote: Third, I got Nebius, which I actually shared at $60 and ever since it's been on a crazy run-up, but I still think we're just getting started. And 10 years from now, these prices are gonna look cheap. They're an AI infrastructure company, by the way.
calls.summary: The creator is bullish on Nebius as an AI infrastructure company, believing the stock is still early despite past gains.

calls.quote: And for my next pick, I know I say I don't buy into hyped up stocks, but I actually researched Iran and it's crazy. They're a data center company for AI and there is just incredible demand. I think the stock's just gonna keep going higher.
calls.summary: The creator is bullish on IREN (likely Iris Energy) as an AI data center company with strong demand, expecting the stock to rise.

calls.quote: And for my last pick, I'm gonna go high risk, high reward, and go with Root, which is an insurance company. Their market cap is only $1 billion and based on fundamentals, like their financial statements, they are severely undervalued right now.
calls.summary: The creator is bullish on Root as a high-risk, high-reward insurance company that is severely undervalued based on fundamentals.

calls.quote: I am not selling any shares, especially after this news. I think they're still undervalued.
